s: The grammar pattern からといって means 'just because A it is not enough to B'

d: in the middle of; not yet finished V-ing
To be decided, to be arranged
hesitate to, cannot, hard to do

s: The grammar pattern に当たって; に当たり means 'on the occasion of; at; in; before; prior to'

just because A it is not enough to B
worry about, there is a possibility, there is fear that
e: Pattern: Verb dictionary form+に+あたって/あたり, Noun+に+あたって/あたり. On the occasion of my study abroad, my father told me about his own experience.

e: Pattern: Noun+に加えて. Well, in addition to that, he also paid for the food.

s: The grammar pattern 〜はさておき means 'leaving aside, aside from'

as a result of, because of, due to
compared to, in contrast to, whereas
instead of, in place of, rather than
e: Pattern: Noun+はさておき+.... Leaving that aside

s: The grammar pattern に応えて means 'in response to'

s: The grammar pattern 限り means 'as far as, as long as, until, unless'
q: What does the grammar pattern '限り' mean?
a: as far as, as long as, until, unless
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
on the other hand from this perspective
e: Pattern: Verb casual form+限り, Noun+である+限り, Adjective+限り. as far as I know

s: The grammar pattern どころか means 'far from A, in fact B; not just; even'

a: far from A, in fact B; not just; even
d: No sooner than; just as, at that instant
As much as; As fast as; To the extent of.
almost/nearly; to the point of; like that; such as that
Probable negative plain verbform. As in, "he probably did not go." Same meaning as -nandarou 〜なんだろう.
e: Pattern: N+どころか, い ADJ+どころか, な ADJ (-な)+どころか, V (普通形 but not past)+どころか. Far from being nice, she is in fact an ogre.

s: The grammar pattern にもかかわらず means 'despite; though; although; in spite of'

you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: Pattern: Verb plain form+にもかかわらず, い-adjective+にもかかわらず, な-adjective (without な)+(である)+にもかかわらず, Noun+にもかかわらず. despite the rain lots of people came

s: The grammar pattern 〜につけ means 'each time, whenever, either way'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜につけ' mean?
d: moreover; what's more; additionally; not only
if one did; if one were to
e: whenever i see my mum knitting a sweater..

s: The grammar pattern たび、たびに means 'each time, every time, whenever'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'たび、たびに' mean?
d: as soon as I noticed, almost immediately
for, over, extending, stretching, ranging, covering
e: Pattern: Noun+の+たび+に, Verb (dictionary form)+たび+に. Whenever I go overseas, I realize the merits of my own country.

s: The grammar pattern ことになる means 'To be decided, to be arranged'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ことになる' mean?
a: To be decided, to be arranged
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
you are required to complete this task
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
however and yet despite all this
e: It has been decided I will go to new york

s: The grammar pattern における・おいて means 'at, in, as for'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'における・おいて' mean?
d: covered with; littered with; infested with,full of
e: the progress IN science / occur AT the hotel

s: The grammar pattern かけだ・かけの・かける means 'in the middle of; not yet finished V-ing'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'かけだ・かけの・かける' mean?
a: in the middle of; not yet finished V-ing
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
on the other hand from this perspective
e: Pattern: Verb ます stem+かけだ. I fell asleep while in the middle of reading the magazine.

s: The grammar pattern 得る means 'able to do'
q: What does the grammar pattern '得る' mean?
e: All I can think of

s: The grammar pattern 〜極まる means 'Extremely; The height of…; Knows no bounds. Always has a negative meaning.'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜極まる' mean?
a: Extremely; The height of…; Knows no bounds. Always has a negative meaning.
d: when; on the occasion of; at (the time of); in; upon (doing something)
Show a strong feeling of opposition.
on the occasion of; at; in; before; prior to
e: The height of rudeness.

s: The grammar pattern ような気がする means 'It's like it could be...'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'ような気がする' mean?
d: there is such a thing (essence), feels like
have the knack to deal with something; completely
be dying to do something, to feel X strongly, can't help it...
Vru, since; now that; once; as long as
e: It's like we can make it a Curry and Rice Night

s: The grammar pattern たまらない means '(1) intolerable, unbearable, unendurable, (2) cannot help (doing), cannot but do, anxious to do, (3) tremendous, out of this world, irresistible.'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'たまらない' mean?
a: (1) intolerable, unbearable, unendurable, (2) cannot help (doing), cannot but do, anxious to do, (3) tremendous, out of this world, irresistible.
d: Doesn't mean that; It's not (true) that; Not necessarily
It seems that; I heard that
on the other hand, on the other side, one-way
and so on; and the like; for example; things like 〜
have the knack to deal with something; completely
commensurate with, accordance with, depending on
e: It's unbearably hot today

s: The grammar pattern 一息 means 'a short period of time'
q: What does the grammar pattern '一息' mean?
d: Not only not X, but also not Y. Both can be good things, or both bad.
and so on; and the like; for example; things like 〜
e: Don`t rest now work is almost finished.

s: The grammar pattern 〜をめぐって means 'surrounding, concerning, about [/ focusing on / in connection with]'
q: What does the grammar pattern '〜をめぐって' mean?
a: surrounding, concerning, about [/ focusing on / in connection with]
d: Vm na (positive command - sentence ending particle)
for, over, extending, stretching, ranging, covering
Extremely; The height of…; Knows no bounds. Always has a negative meaning.
e: Pattern: Noun+をめぐって. There is much debate "concerning...etc" ......X

s: The grammar pattern 最中に means 'right in the middle of'
q: What does the grammar pattern '最中に' mean?
d: however and yet despite all this
to repeat the same action over
after a long time has passed
in the not too distant future
e: Pattern: Verb ている+最中+(に), Noun+の+最中+(に), い-adjective+最中+(に). Right in the middle of the ceremony, the lights went out.

s: The grammar pattern はもとより means 'to begin with ... and also ...'
q: What does the grammar pattern 'はもとより' mean?
d: at the exact moment when something happens
then and only then will it occur
it is permitted and allowed to do so
you are required to complete this task
someone is supposed to act in this way
it is absolutely necessary to do it
as a direct result of what happened
consequently and as a logical outcome
e: she speaks english to begin with, but also french and german

s: The grammar pattern 一方だ means 'increasing tendency'
q: What does the grammar pattern '一方だ' mean?
e: Pattern: Ｖる+一方. the number of people with mobile phones is increasing
